steps.title: Identify what would weaken the claim
steps.content: The student claims that emotionality of movie reviews is unrelated to box office success. To weaken this, we need a finding showing that emotionality does predict box office earnings — a direct link between the two variables.

steps.title: Test each choice against that standard
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Movies with highest emotionality scores received lowest star ratings': connects emotionality to star ratings, not to box office earnings — irrelevant to the student's claim. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Emotionality score was a positive predictor of box office earnings': directly links emotionality to box office success, contradicting the student's claim that the two are unrelated. ✓
  • Choice C — 'More than half of movies received an average star rating of 3 out of 5': describes star ratings only; says nothing about emotionality or its relationship to earnings. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Most successful movies had high average star ratings': links star ratings to box office success, but does not address emotionality at all. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
